question.text: When comparing mutual fund schemes, it is most meaningful to compare __________.

answer.text: Schemes within the same category (e.g., large-cap with large-cap)
Any equity scheme with any debt scheme
A liquid fund with a small-cap equity fund
A gold fund with a banking sector fund

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A fair comparison can only be made between schemes of the same category. Comparing a large-cap equity fund with another large-cap equity fund is meaningful. Comparing a debt fund with an equity fund is like comparing apples with oranges — they have different risk profiles and investment objectives.
**The Trap:** Comparing across different categories is misleading. A liquid fund will always have lower returns than an equity fund, but that does not make it a bad fund — it simply has a different purpose.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Same-category comparison is a fundamental principle of performance evaluation.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When an investor asks "which is the best fund?", always clarify: "Best in which category?" Help them compare within the same category for a meaningful evaluation.
